This letter serves as a formal authorization recognizing M/s DUGONG INTERNATIONAL PVT. LTD. as an authorized Original
Equipment Manufacturer (OEM). This certificate confirms our capability and authority to produce and supply of Desktop Computers and
All In One PC  as per the industry standards and requirements as also confirmed by QCI certification. As an OEM, we are fully equipped
to design, manufacture, and supply Desktop Computers  and All In One PC and adhere to the highest standards of quality and
compliance as set forth by relevant industry standards or regulatory bodies. Additionally, we are available to provide on-site services
throughout pan India as well.
We are committed to maintaining the highest standards in our manufacturing processes and to continually enhancing our product
offerings. We value our role as an OEM and are dedicated to contributing positively to the Government e-Marketplace (GeM).

Undertaking of Genuine Preloaded Operating System
We, M/s. DUGONG INTERNATIONAL PVT. LTD., are committed to providing you with preloaded Windows
with genuine with Licenses/Keys  and authentic operating systems. To ensure the authenticity of our products, we
employ rigorous quality control measures and source our software directly from reputable, authorized distributors.
Additionally, each purchase is accompanied by a unique serial number and a certificate of authenticity to guarantee
the legitimacy of the operating system.
In the rare event that you suspect any irregularities with the product, please don't hesitate to contact our dedicated
customer support team, who will promptly address your concerns. Your trust in our products is of utmost
importance to us, and we remain dedicated to delivering reliable and genuine operating systems.

OEM LOGO [MOTHERBOARD] DECLARATION 
 
 
As per Rule 144(iii) of GFR 2017,  
(i) The technical specifications shall, to the extent practicable, be based on the national technical 
regulations or recognized national standards.  
(ii) A motherboard's logo and other internal components are not inherently mandatory for its 
functionality. The logo, often bearing the branding of the manufacturer or OEM (Original 
Equipment Manufacturer),  
(iii) Serves primarily as an aesthetic and branding element. It holds no direct bearing on the core 
operations or performance of the motherboard.  
Similarly, other internal components, while essential for the motherboard's overall functionality, 
do not rely on specific logos or visual markings to operate efficiently. Therefore, the absence or 
alteration of these visual elements, while potentially impacting the product's marketability and 
brand recognition, does not impede the motherboard's primary functionality or the performance 
of its integrated parts.
